ai.name: AI & Tech
ai.desc: Models · agent tooling · research · industry moves

stories.head: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in Nasdaq debut — biggest-ever foreign first-time US listing
stories.brief: SK Hynix's Wall Street debut raises $26.5B; ADRs priced at $149 close at $168.01.
stories.tldr: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in its Wall Street debut, the biggest first-time listing by a foreign company in the US [1]. Its American depositary receipts, priced at $149, finished their first New York session at $168.01 each [1].
stories.why: As a semiconductor and data-center investor, this is a fresh, liquid US-listed way to play the HBM leader driving the AI-memory cycle — watch how the ADR trades against Samsung and Micron.

sources.excerpt: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in its Wall Street debut, marking the biggest first-time listing by a foreign company in the US. The company's American depositary receipts were priced at $149, and finished their first session on Friday in New York at $168.01 each.

stories.head: Samsung guides to record Q2 — ~89.4T won profit — yet shares fall nearly 7%
stories.brief: Samsung guides Q2 profit near 89.4T won, but shares close almost 7% lower.
stories.tldr: Samsung Electronics guided to approximately 171 trillion won in Q2 consolidated sales and approximately 89.4 trillion won in operating profit [1]. Shares still closed nearly 7% lower Tuesday, as capex and demand concerns clouded the outlook despite the record preliminary profit [2].
stories.why: As a semiconductor investor, the tell here is the paradox: record HBM/DRAM-driven guidance met by a selloff on capex and AI-demand worries — a sentiment gauge for your memory-cycle thesis.

sources.excerpt: Consolidated Sales: Approximately 171 trillion Korean won · Consolidated Operating Profit: Approximately 89.4 trillion Korean won · The above estimates are based on K-IFRS. Please note that Korean disclosure regulations do not allow earnings estimates to be offered as a range.
Samsung Electronics shares fell even after record preliminary second-quarter profit as capex and demand concerns clouded the outlook. Shares closed nearly 7% lower Tuesday

stories.head: US chip stocks shed over $1 trillion as Wall Street doubts AI-capex durability
stories.brief: Semiconductor stocks lose over $1 trillion as AI-spending sustainability is questioned.
stories.tldr: US semiconductor stocks wiped out over a trillion dollars in market value as Wall Street questioned the sustainability of record AI capital spending [1].
stories.why: As a semiconductors and data-center investor, this is the drawdown that stress-tests your whole AI-infrastructure book — the question is whether the capex-ROI doubt is a dip or a regime change.

sources.excerpt: Semiconductor stocks are experiencing a sharp downturn, wiping out over a trillion dollars in market value, as Wall Street questions the sustainability of record AI capital spending.

stories.head: FOMC minutes: a few officials made the case for a June hike but backed a hold
stories.brief: June FOMC minutes: a few members argued to hike, yet all supported holding rates.
stories.tldr: The June FOMC minutes show a few participants commented there was a case for raising the target range for the federal funds rate, but those participants indicated they supported maintaining the current target range at the meeting [1].
stories.why: As a rate-sensitive investor in semis, data-center, and energy, this hawkish dissent shapes the discount rate on your growth names — a higher-for-longer tilt pressures the very AI-infrastructure trade you track.

sources.handle: Board of Governors, Federal Reserve System
sources.excerpt: A few participants commented that, in light of these developments, there was a case for raising the target range for the federal funds rate, but those participants indicated that they supported maintaining the current target range at this meeting.

stories.head: Google's AlphaEvolve code-optimization agent hits general availability on Gemini platform
stories.brief: AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-based code-optimization agent, is now generally available.
stories.tldr: Google Cloud made AlphaEvolve generally available on its Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform [1]. It is a code optimization and discovery agent built on top of Gemini, aimed at solving the hardest algorithmic problems for business and research [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building AI agents, this is a direct competitive reference point for Claude-based agent tooling — worth benchmarking AlphaEvolve's evolutionary code-optimization loop against your own stack.

sources.excerpt: Today, AlphaEvolve is generally available (GA) on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. AlphaEvolve is a code optimization and discovery agent built on top of Gemini that helps solve the hardest algorithmic problems and achieve breakthroughs for your business and research.

stories.head: Brent crude settles up 3% at $74.16 a barrel
stories.brief: International oil benchmark climbs 3% on the session to $74.16.
stories.tldr: Brent crude futures, the international benchmark, settled 3% higher at $74.16 per barrel [1].
stories.why: As an energy-focused investor, a firming crude benchmark feeds directly into your energy-theme positioning and the input-cost backdrop for the power-hungry data-center trade you also track.

sources.excerpt: Brent crude futures, the international benchmark, settled 3% higher at $74.16 per barrel.

stories.head: Bank Negara holds OPR at 2.75% — KLCI dips 0.35% as tech stocks cushion losses
stories.brief: BNM keeps OPR at 2.75%; KLCI slips to 1,677.64 as chip counters cushion the fall.
stories.tldr: Bank Negara Malaysia's Monetary Policy Committee decided to maintain the Overnight Policy Rate at 2.75% at its July 9 meeting [1]. The FBM KLCI closed 5.97 points, or 0.35%, lower at 1,677.64, though gains in semiconductor and technology stocks helped cushion losses [2].
stories.why: As a Bursa investor, this is your home rate decision and index print in one: rates steady, and the semiconductor/tech rotation cushioning the KLCI mirrors the same theme you follow across US chips.

sources.excerpt: At its meeting on July 9, 2026, the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of Bank Negara Malaysia decided to maintain the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) at 2.75 percent.
FBM KLCI closing 5.97 points or 0.35% lower at 1,677.64, though gains in semiconductor and technology stocks helped cushion losses

stories.head: NVIDIA spokesperson: 'Our roadmap remains intact'
stories.brief: NVIDIA states its product roadmap remains intact, a company spokesperson says.
stories.tldr: An NVIDIA company spokesperson said, 'Our roadmap remains intact' [1].
stories.why: As a semiconductor investor, NVIDIA's roadmap cadence anchors the entire AI-server supply chain — a direct reassurance on timing is a signal worth weighing against the chip-selloff narrative.

sources.excerpt: a company spokesperson said, 'Our roadmap remains intact.'

stories.head: Godot 4.8 opens with its first development snapshot for early testing
stories.brief: Godot 4.8's first dev snapshot lands for early testing and feature evaluation.
stories.tldr: Godot 4.8 dev 1, the first development snapshot for the 4.8 feature release, is now available [1]. It is an in-development build provided for early testing and feature evaluation while the engine is still being worked on [1].
stories.why: As a UE5 developer, Godot's release cadence is a useful pulse on the broader engine ecosystem — worth a look if you evaluate lighter-weight tooling alongside your Nanite/materials work.

sources.excerpt: Godot 4.8 dev 1 is a dev snapshot for the 4.8 feature release. Dev snapshots are in-development builds of the engine provided for early testing and feature evaluation while the engine is still being worked on.

stories.head: Anthropic adds API key expiration to Claude Console — with pre-expiry email reminders
stories.brief: Claude Console now supports API key expiry, emailing creators before keys lapse.
stories.tldr: Anthropic now lets you set an expiration when creating an API key or Admin API key in the Claude Console, choosing a preset, a custom duration, or Never [1]. For keys with a lifetime of at least 7 days, Anthropic emails the creator before expiration, existing keys are unaffected, and each key's expiration appears in the Admin API's expires_at field [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building agents on Claude, this is directly actionable credential hygiene — you can now scope and auto-expire keys per agent deployment and get warned before one lapses.

sources.excerpt: You can now set an expiration when you create an API key or an Admin API key in the Claude Console. Choose a preset, a custom duration, or Never. For keys with a lifetime of at least 7 days, Anthropic emails the creator before expiration. Existing keys are unaffected. The Admin API reports each key's expiration in the expires_at field.

stories.head: Google Cloud Run Sandboxes — ultra-fast runtime for untrusted agent code, starting in milliseconds
stories.brief: Cloud Run Sandboxes run untrusted code and agent workloads, starting in milliseconds.
stories.tldr: Google Cloud Run sandboxes are a native, secure, and ultra-fast runtime environment built specifically for executing untrusted code and agent workloads, starting in milliseconds [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building AI agents, this is a ready-made isolation primitive for running LLM-generated code safely — a pattern you may want to match or adopt in your own Claude-based agent stack.

sources.excerpt: Cloud Run sandboxes are a native, secure, and ultra-fast runtime environment built specifically for executing untrusted code and agent workloads, starting in milliseconds.

stories.head: Google Cloud survey: 83% of organizations need infrastructure upgrades for agentic AI
stories.brief: 83% of 1,400+ IT leaders say infra upgrades are needed for production agentic AI.
stories.tldr: In a Google Cloud survey of more than 1,400 senior IT leaders, 83% of organizations said they require infrastructure upgrades to support production-grade agentic AI [1].
stories.why: This straddles both your hats: as an agent builder it flags the infra gap you'll hit at production scale, and as a data-center investor it quantifies the upgrade demand underpinning the buildout thesis.

sources.excerpt: We recently surveyed more than 1,400 senior IT leaders for our State of AI Infrastructure report, and a resounding pattern emerged: the gap between AI ambition and infrastructure reality is widening. In fact, 83% of organizations say they require infrastructure upgrades to support production-grade agentic AI.

stories.head: Google Cloud guide shows vendors how to sell agents as a service on its Marketplace
stories.brief: New guide lets vendors publish and monetize AI agents on Google Cloud Marketplace.
stories.tldr: Google Cloud published a guide for building agents for Gemini Enterprise and Google Cloud Marketplace as an AI Agent-as-a-Service solution, framed as a path to get an agent into the daily workflow of millions of enterprise users [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building AI agents, this maps out a concrete distribution and monetization channel — useful context if you ever consider packaging your agent work as a commercial service.

sources.excerpt: Building agents for Gemini Enterprise and Google Cloud Marketplace as an AI Agent-as-a-Service solution provides a path to extend your reach and to get your agent into the daily workflow of millions of enterprise users.

stories.head: Google Cloud team: a single pass/fail benchmark score misrepresents AI-agent capability
stories.brief: Google researchers argue a fixed benchmark score is a poor gauge of agent ability.
stories.tldr: Google Cloud researchers argue that running a fixed benchmark, calculating a score, and declaring progress is exactly how AI agents are evaluated — and that 'a passing grade is the least interesting thing an exam can tell you' [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building agents, this reframes how you should measure your own systems — a caution that single-number benchmarks can mask where a Claude-based agent actually breaks.

sources.excerpt: A passing grade is the least interesting thing an exam can tell you...Yet this is exactly how we evaluate AI agents. We run a fixed benchmark, calculate a score, and declare progress.

stories.head: soft-ue-cli v1.42.0 patches JSON key conversion, keeping UE 5.7 compatibility
stories.brief: AI-driven UE control tool's v1.42.0 fixes a JSON key-conversion issue, keeps UE 5.7.
stories.tldr: soft-ue-cli v1.42.0 removes the direct FJsonObject::FStringType dependency from JSON object key conversion while preserving UE 5.7 compatibility [1].
stories.why: As a UE5 engineer driving Unreal from AI agents like Claude Code, this MCP/CLI tool sits in your workflow — the fix keeps its JSON bridge working as you move between Unreal versions.

sources.excerpt: removing the direct `FJsonObject::FStringType` dependency from JSON object key conversion while preserving UE 5.7 compatibility

rejected.reason: Excerpt covers only the general product description and does not substantiate specific claim details: version v2.3, July 11 release date, or simultaneous three-UE-version packaging are absent from the excerpt; dynamic releases/latest URL does not lock to v2.3; confidence 0.52 below 0.6 gate.
Article accessible (July 7) but WebFetch returned a model-generated summary rather than raw article text; no verbatim excerpt identifiable with confidence; dropped per excerpt-gate rule.
